course.title: Lesson 18: Electric Currents in Modern Art

segments.text: To notice examples of modern sculpture on display in public places.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们很容易注意到公共场所展出的现代雕塑。

segments.text: forms stand in gardens and outside buildings and shops.

translations.zh-Hans: 各种形状的作品矗立在花园里以及楼房和商店外。

segments.text: We have got quite used to them.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们已经相当习惯它们了。

segments.text: Some so-called modern pieces have been on display for nearly 80 years.

translations.zh-Hans: 有些所谓的现代作品已经展出了近80年。

segments.text: In spite of this, some people, including myself, were surprised by a recent exhibition of modern sculpture.

translations.zh-Hans: 尽管如此，最近一次现代雕塑展还是令包括我在内的一些人大吃一惊。

segments.text: The first thing I saw when I entered the art gallery was a notice which said, Do not touch the exhibits.

translations.zh-Hans: 走进美术馆后，我首先看到一则告示，上面写着：“请勿触摸展品。”

segments.text: The objects on display were pieces of moving sculpture.

translations.zh-Hans: 展出的作品都是会动的雕塑。

segments.text: Oddly shaped forms that are suspended from the ceiling and move, in response to a gust of wind, are quite familiar to everybody.

translations.zh-Hans: 人们都很熟悉那些悬在天花板上、会随风摆动的奇形怪状物体。
然而，这里的展品却不一样。

segments.text: Lined up against the wall, there were long, thin wires attached to metal spheres.

translations.zh-Hans: 靠墙排列着细长的金属丝，末端连着金属球。

segments.text: The spheres had been magnetized and attracted or repelled each other all the time.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些球经过磁化，始终相互吸引或排斥。

segments.text: In the center of the hall, there were a number of tall structures which contained colored lights.

translations.zh-Hans: 大厅中央有许多装着彩灯的高大结构。

segments.text: These lights flickered continuously, like traffic lights which have gone mad.

translations.zh-Hans: 灯光不停闪烁，仿佛发了疯的交通信号灯。

segments.text: were emitted from small black boxes and red lamps flashed on and off angrily.

translations.zh-Hans: 声音从小黑盒子里发出，红灯愤怒地忽明忽灭。

segments.text: It was rather like an exhibition of prehistoric electronic equipment.

translations.zh-Hans: 这里颇像一个史前电子设备展览。

segments.text: These peculiar forms not only seemed designed to shock people emotionally, but to give them electric shocks as well.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些奇特的造型似乎不仅为了在情感上震撼观众，还要让他们真正遭受电击。
